response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering fundamentals with 19+ years of experience, solid agile practices, and excellent problem-solving abilities, there are critical gaps that make them unsuitable for this mid-senior C++ systems programming role. Most significantly, documented C++ experience is minimal (only 6 months at National Instruments), the career trajectory is heavily weighted toward web/JavaScript/TypeScript development rather than systems programming, and there is complete absence of industrial cybersecurity expertise (IEC 62443, SDL practices) which is a core responsibility. The skill set mismatch is too fundamental to overcome through ramp-up.
strengths: Extensive software development experience (19+ years) with solid CS degree and demonstrated architectural thinking across diverse platforms
Strong agile engineering culture with explicit TDD, CI/CD, and software craftsmanship practices proven at multiple senior roles
Proven mentoring and technical leadership through organizing refactoring dojos, public workshops, and significant open-source contributions
concerns: Critical C++ expertise gap: only 6 months documented C++ experience despite it being the primary technical requirement; career heavily focused on JavaScript/TypeScript/DLang web services
Complete absence of industrial/cybersecurity domain knowledge: no mention of IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, or industrial automation experience which are core responsibilities
Wrong technical domain: career optimized for web and backend services rather than systems programming, networking, multi-threading, and embedded/on-premises solutions

key_factors.factor: Industrial cybersecurity and SDL knowledge gap
Agile methodology and software craftsmanship alignment
